violations.text: Milk and cream in thermos dispensers observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
Employee observed drying food container with a towel. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the front service area. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit (especially the lid), cream cheese unit, and blender base have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer is to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Cut tomatoes and cheese observed held at 48-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was placed in refrigerator. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. Liquid observed in stacked bowls on clean storage rack. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC has signed up 2 staff members to take a food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2025

Dishes were observed drying on top of large plastic lids on the drying rack with water accumulating on the lids. Dishes must be allowed to air dry properly. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the storage racks in the kitchen area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2025
Ice was observed in an ice maker with debris. PIC discarded ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned & sanitized, and bagged ice was provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Cut tomatoes were observed held at between 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed of tomatoes. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Establishment does not currently have a variance for specialty bagels being left out at room temperature. Such bagels include pizza (tomato sauce, cheese, peperoni), triple cheese, jalapeno cheddar, and spinach. Establishment may apply for time for a public health, hold bagels under refrigeration, or test their specialty bagels by a food authority proving these bagels are shelf stable. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Bagels packaged in the food establishment, and sold as self service, are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name and address. PIC moved the bagged bagels behind the counter. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
Per interview, single use containers are reused to store food product. PIC discarded containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Wood is used in the construction of a shelf between the prep unit and prep table in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Linens are used to line the clean storage rack. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
The following items were not in good repair: -slicer, taken out of service by PIC, -air curtains in the walk-in cooler, -wood bagel boards (with splintering). New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-mixers,-dry ingredient storage bins,-prep units,-safe used as a table for the mixer,-utensil drawer,-cleaned containers (sticker residue),-rolling racks. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
The door to the kitchen toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

The floors (including the floors in the walk-in freezer) throughout the facility, and air vent fan in the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris.The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
The pass through service window in between the kitchen and service linen are in poor repair.The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
Some ceiling tiles in the toilet/mop room observed in poor repair, stained with previous water damage. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
